title: Human influenza A virus H1N1 in marine mammals in California, 2019
author: Coffey, Lark
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
From 2011-2018, we conducted surveillance in marine mammals along the California 

 coast for influenza A virus (IAV), frequently detecting anti-influenza antibodies and 

 intermittently detecting IAV. In spring 2019, this pattern changed. Despite no change in 

 surveillance intensity, we detected IAV RNA in 10 samples in March and April, mostly 

 in nasal and rectal swabs from northern elephant seals (Mirounga 

 angustirostris). Although virus isolation was unsuccessful, IAV sequenced from one 

 northern elephant seal nasal swab showed close genetic identity with pandemic H1N1 

 IAV subclade 6B.1A.1 that was concurrently circulating in humans in the 2018/19 

 influenza season. This represents the first report of human A(H1N1)pdm09 IAV in 

 northern elephant seals since 2010, suggesting IAV continues to spill over from 

 humans to pinnipeds.
